title: React Re-render
body:
## Definition

A **re-render** is when React calls a component function again to produce a new element tree because inputs changed or a parent re-rendered.

## Why it exists

UI must reflect current props, state, and context.

## How it works

Triggers: [useState](concept:use-state)/[useReducer](concept:use-reducer) dispatch, parent re-render, [context](concept:use-context) value change, store subscription. Re-render ≠ DOM update — [reconciliation](concept:reconciliation) may skip DOM changes.

## Common confusion

Child re-renders when parent renders even if props unchanged — unless memoized. Context updates re-render all consumers.

## In practice

Profile with React DevTools; optimize with composition, memo, stable props — not premature everywhere.
bodyLinked:
## Definition

A **[re-render](concept:react-re-render)** is when React [calls](concept:call-apply-bind) a component function again to produce a [new](concept:new-operator) element tree because inputs changed or a parent re-rendered.

## Why it exists

UI must reflect current props, state, and [context](concept:use-context).

## How it works

Triggers: [useState](concept:use-state)/[useReducer](concept:use-reducer) dispatch, parent [re-render](concept:react-re-render), [context](concept:use-context) value change, store subscription. [Re-render](concept:react-re-render) ≠ [DOM](concept:dom) update — [reconciliation](concept:reconciliation) may skip [DOM](concept:dom) changes.

## Common confusion

Child [re-renders](concept:react-re-render) when parent renders even if props unchanged — unless memoized. [Context](concept:use-context) updates [re-render](concept:react-re-render) all consumers.

## In practice

Profile with [React DevTools](concept:js-debugging-tools); optimize with [composition](concept:react-composition), [memo](concept:react-memo), stable props — not premature everywhere.
